symptom_description: Loud intermittent screeching, grinding, and buzzing/humming sound emanating from carry-side troughing idler roller near Station 14 (`55 Hz Hz` signature).
root_cause: Incorrect bearing tolerance (`0.004`) led to inner race spinning on the shaft, causing friction welding and bearing lockup.
action_taken: Replaced seized rollers (`P/N 7348-A`), installed deflector shields (`P/N 5791-X`) to prevent washdown water entry, and verified tracking.
parts_used: CEMA E Rollers, Water Deflector Shields, Mounting Hardware

symptom_description: Edge damage (`0.14 inch` chunk missing) on the belt. The belt is folded over on the return side (`56%` folded).
root_cause: Improper tension adjustment on gravity screw take-up assembly (`73 ft-lbs` uneven tension between left and right take-up bearings).
action_taken: Installed 3 self-aligning training return idlers (`P/N 3578-REV2`) along return span, cleaned material off tail pulley wing rolls, squared take-up bearings, and verified belt centered under full load.
parts_used: Heavy-Duty Return Training Roller Assembly (2-Pack), Take-up Bearing Adjusting Bolt Kit

symptom_description: Thermal inspection (`IR gun`) showed seized return idler bearing reaching `86`°C. Sparks visible (`sharp` hazard) under dark operating conditions. High load demand from driven equipment (MOTOR-602) suspected of accelerating component wear.
root_cause: Normal fatigue failure and spalling of internal idler ball bearings after exceeding `6` hours of continuous operating life under heavy impact loading.
action_taken: Replaced 4 seized return idlers (`P/N 2996-A`) and 2 impact bed idlers under transfer point, cleared spilled material (`6.1 lbs`) from under structure, and verified smooth, quiet rotation.
parts_used: Heavy-Duty Impact Troughing Idler Assemblies (Set of 2), Return Rollers (4-Pack), Idler Mounting Brackets

symptom_description: Drive motor drawing continuous `127` Amps (`115%` rating). Gearbox output shaft rotating sluggishly with severe laboring noise (`rumbling`).
root_cause: Loss of lagging (`20%` missing) on the drive pulley causing slip. The control system ramps up speed (`22 Hz Hz`) to compensate, overloading the motor.
action_taken: Re-lagged drive pulley (`P/N 1961-A`) with diamond tread rubber (`0.10 inch` thick), tightened take-up (`49 ft-lbs` tension), and verified zero slip with tachometer.
parts_used: Ceramic Lagging Strips, Cold Vulcanizing Adhesive, Tensioning Bolts

symptom_description: VFD fault code indicating over-current (`175 A`). Motor smells of burnt varnish (`285 ppm` VOCs).
root_cause: Tail pulley or take-up assembly jammed with packed solid material (`2.7 lbs`), creating immense frictional drag against belt movement.
action_taken: Cleared mechanical jam inside head pulley discharge chute, re-torqued motor terminal connections (`244 in-lbs`), checked gearbox oil level, and verified VFD current stability across full speed range (`55 Hz Hz`).
parts_used: Thermal Overload Relay Element, Take-up Slide Lubricant (1 Can), Troughing Idler Rollers (4-Pack)

symptom_description: Conveyor drive motor tripped VFD/thermal overload relay (`motor current spiked to 207 Amps vs 32A FLA`). Drive pulley stationary while motor humming.
root_cause: Loss of lagging (`83%` missing) on the drive pulley causing slip. The control system ramps up speed (`24 Hz Hz`) to compensate, overloading the motor.
action_taken: Re-lagged drive pulley (`P/N 9531-X`) with diamond tread rubber (`0.12 inch` thick), tightened take-up (`319 in-lbs` tension), and verified zero slip with tachometer.
parts_used: Ceramic Lagging Strips, Cold Vulcanizing Adhesive, Tensioning Bolts

symptom_description: Conveyor belt mistracking severely (`drastic` off-center toward right side). Belt edge rubbing against steel conveyor frame (`chattering` squealing sound and rubber dust). Also inspected MOTOR-602 to rule out secondary mechanical strain or electrical feed anomalies.
root_cause: Uneven loading (`26%` biased to one side) from the feed chute pushing the belt off-center.
action_taken: Cut out crooked mechanical splice, re-squared belt ends using transit (`moderate` tolerance), and installed new mechanical fasteners (`P/N 4331-A`).
parts_used: Mechanical Splice Kit, Belt Squaring Tool, Nylon Splice Pin

symptom_description: Conveyor belt mistracking severely (`progressive` off-center toward right side). Belt edge rubbing against steel conveyor frame (`high-pitched squealing` squealing sound and rubber dust). Tech observed abnormal acoustic noise propagating across the coupling from MOTOR-602.
root_cause: Material buildup (`0.10 inch` sticky wet clay/bulk product) frozen or caked onto head pulley and return idler rolls, creating uneven diameter and forcing belt to track to one side.
action_taken: Locked out conveyor, manually scraped and cleaned hardened material buildup (`4.9 lbs`) from head pulley and return idlers, adjusted take-up screw tension equally (`326 in-lbs` torque), and test ran belt to track center.
parts_used: Self-Aligning Training Idlers (Set of 3), Return Idler Rollers (4-Pack)

symptom_description: Belt edge torn and fraying (`25%` internal fabric carcass cords exposed and catching on conveyor frame brackets).
root_cause: Mechanical fastener/lacing splice pulled apart (`59%` tension) and caught on fixed belt scraper blade, ripping belt edge open.
action_taken: Performed hot vulcanized splice repair (`89°C`) on torn belt section, removed jammed tramp metal (`3.5 lbs`) from transfer chute, and installed new heavy-duty urethane skirt board sealing strips.
parts_used: Hot Vulcanizing Splice Kit & Gum Rubber, Primary Belt Cleaner Scraper Blade, Urethane Skirt Strip

symptom_description: High torque alarm active on conveyor drive controller. Motor frame temperature `123`°C (`concerning` overheating). Conveyor struggling to start under load. Issue first noticed after high-temperature trip occurred on MOTOR-602.
root_cause: Conveyor overloaded beyond maximum design capacity (`surge loading from upstream bin discharge exceeding 84% TPH`).
action_taken: Cleared excess surge material load (`2.2 lbs`) from belt carrying surface, freed up 6 jammed idler rollers under skirt board, reset motor thermal overload relay, and verified normal running draw (`112 Amps`).
parts_used: Fluid Coupling Thermal Fuse Plug 140°C, ISO VG 32 Fluid Coupling Oil (2 Gallons), Replacement Return Idlers (4-Pack)

symptom_description: Conveyor belt slipping on the drive pulley (`35%` slip). Motor running at `57 Hz Hz` but belt speed is zero. Ops reported load fluctuations on MOTOR-602 coinciding with this fault.
root_cause: Loss of lagging (`17%` missing) on the drive pulley causing slip. The control system ramps up speed (`22 Hz Hz`) to compensate, overloading the motor.
action_taken: Re-lagged drive pulley (`Spec-897`) with diamond tread rubber (`0.05 inch` thick), tightened take-up (`108 ft-lbs` tension), and verified zero slip with tachometer.
parts_used: Ceramic Lagging Strips, Cold Vulcanizing Adhesive, Tensioning Bolts

symptom_description: Mechanical belt rip detector interlock tripped, stopping conveyor immediately. Inspection revealed persistent puncture hole (`6.6 inch` diameter) through entire belt thickness.
root_cause: Chemical degradation (`pH 8.5`) softened the top cover, allowing normal material impact to tear the rubber (`69%` degradation).
action_taken: Stitched longitudinal tear (`5.1 ft`) with rip repair fasteners (`P/N 3922-C`), replaced worn skirt rubber (`P/N 1029-C`), and recalibrated rip detector.
parts_used: Rip Repair Fasteners (Box of 100), Skirtboard Rubber (50ft), Fastener Template

symptom_description: Longitudinal rip (`5.0 ft` length) gouged into top rubber cover of conveyor belt near loading transfer chute.
root_cause: Sharp piece of tramp metal (`P/N 2753-X`) or structural steel bracket broke off and wedged between skirt board and belt surface at loading chute, gouging belt during transit.
action_taken: Locked out unit, trimmed frayed belt edges, repaired 4-foot longitudinal top cover gouge using cold-cure urethane repair compound (`P/N 9031-A`) and rubber repair strips, and readjusted skirt board clamps.
parts_used: Cold-Cure Urethane Belt Repair Compound Kit (2 Packs), Heavy-Duty Rubber Patch Strips, Urethane Skirt Board Sealing Rubber (20ft)

symptom_description: Ops noted conveyor belt wandering back and forth (`18 Hz cycles/min`) across troughing idlers during load surges. Heavy material spillage (`noticeable`) accumulating under carry side.
root_cause: Improper tension adjustment on gravity screw take-up assembly (`54 ft-lbs` uneven tension between left and right take-up bearings).
action_taken: Installed 3 self-aligning training return idlers (`P/N 4483-B`) along return span, cleaned material off tail pulley wing rolls, squared take-up bearings, and verified belt centered under full load.
parts_used: Heavy-Duty Return Training Roller Assembly (2-Pack), Take-up Bearing Adjusting Bolt Kit

symptom_description: Belt splice pulling apart (`0.028 inch` gap). Fasteners tearing through the belt carcass (`49%` failure).
root_cause: Mechanical fastener/lacing splice pulled apart (`35%` tension) and caught on fixed belt scraper blade, ripping belt edge open.
action_taken: Performed hot vulcanized splice repair (`102°C`) on torn belt section, removed jammed tramp metal (`3.6 lbs`) from transfer chute, and installed new heavy-duty urethane skirt board sealing strips.
parts_used: Hot Vulcanizing Splice Kit & Gum Rubber, Primary Belt Cleaner Scraper Blade, Urethane Skirt Strip

symptom_description: Thermal inspection (`IR gun`) showed seized return idler bearing reaching `101`°C. Sparks visible (`marked` hazard) under dark operating conditions.
root_cause: Incorrect bearing tolerance (`0.007`) led to inner race spinning on the shaft, causing friction welding and bearing lockup.
action_taken: Replaced seized rollers (`Spec-227`), installed deflector shields (`P/N 2745-B`) to prevent washdown water entry, and verified tracking.
parts_used: CEMA E Rollers, Water Deflector Shields, Mounting Hardware

symptom_description: Longitudinal rip (`3.9 ft` length) gouged into top rubber cover of conveyor belt near loading transfer chute.
root_cause: Belt slipped off pulley due to misalignment and caught on a rigid structural support (`0.61` stress), tearing the edge.
action_taken: Pulled `2.2 ft` of new belt, performed a hot vulcanized splice (`80°C`), adjusted primary and secondary scrapers, and ran belt unloaded.
parts_used: Conveyor Belt Roll (100ft), Hot Splice Kit, Primary Cleaner Blade
